lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਸੇਵਾ ਬਿਨੁ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਵੀ ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਚੀਨਸਿ ਆਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: gur sevaa bin bhagat na hovee kiau kar cheenas aapai |
lines.english: Without serving the Guru, there is no devotional worship. How can anyone, by himself, know the Lord?

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਅਗਨਿ ਨ ਗੁਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਬੂਝੈ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਪੂਅਰ ਤਾਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: antar agan na gur bin boojhai baahar pooar taapai |
lines.english: Without the Guru, the fire within is not quenched; and outside, the fire still burns.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਠਸਠਿ ਤੀਰਥ ਭਰਮਿ ਵਿਗੂਚਹਿ ਕਿਉ ਮਲੁ ਧੋਪੈ ਪਾਪੈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: atthasatth teerath bharam vigoocheh kiau mal dhopai paapai |3|
lines.english: Wandering to the eixty-eight sacred shrines of pilgrimage, he is ruined. How can the filth of sin be washed away? ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿੰਦਾ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਨਰਕ ਨਿਵਾਸੀ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਆਤਮ ਜਾਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: nindaa kar kar narak nivaasee antar aatam jaapai |
lines.english: Slandering others, one lives in hell; within him is hazy darkness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਛਾਣੀ ਖਾਕੁ ਬਿਭੂਤ ਚੜਾਈ ਮਾਇਆ ਕਾ ਮਗੁ ਜੋਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: chhaanee khaak bibhoot charraaee maaeaa kaa mag johai |
lines.english: He sifts through the dust, and applies ashes to his body, but he is searching for the path of Maya's wealth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਏਕੁ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਸਾਚੁ ਕਹੇ ਤੇ ਛੋਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: antar baahar ek na jaanai saach kahe te chhohai |
lines.english: Inwardly and outwardly, he does not know the One Lord; if someone tells him the Truth, he grows angry.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਠੁ ਪੜੈ ਮੁਖਿ ਝੂਠੋ ਬੋਲੈ ਨਿਗੁਰੇ ਕੀ ਮਤਿ ਓਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: paatth parrai mukh jhoottho bolai nigure kee mat ohai |
lines.english: He reads the scriptures, but tells lies; such is the intellect of one who has no guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਜਪਈ ਕਿਉ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਵੈ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਕਿਉ ਸੋਹੈ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: naam na japee kiau sukh paavai bin naavai kiau sohai |4|
lines.english: Without chanting the Naam, how can he find peace? Without the Name, how can he look good? ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂੰਡੁ ਮੁਡਾਇ ਜਟਾ ਸਿਖ ਬਾਧੀ ਮੋਨਿ ਰਹੈ ਅਭਿਮਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: moondd muddaae jattaa sikh baadhee mon rahai abhimaanaa |
lines.english: Some shave their heads, some keep their hair in matted tangles; some keep it in braids, while some keep silent, filled with egotistical pride.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੂਆ ਡੋਲੈ ਦਹ ਦਿਸ ਧਾਵੈ ਬਿਨੁ ਰਤ ਆਤਮ ਗਿਆਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: manooaa ddolai deh dis dhaavai bin rat aatam giaanaa |
lines.english: Their minds waver and wander in ten directions, without loving devotion and enlightenment of the soul.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਛੋਡਿ ਮਹਾ ਬਿਖੁ ਪੀਵੈ ਮਾਇਆ ਕਾ ਦੇਵਾਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: amrit chhodd mahaa bikh peevai maaeaa kaa devaanaa |
lines.english: They abandon the Ambrosial Nectar, and drink the deadly poison, driven mad by Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਾਥ ਕਮੰਡਲੁ ਕਾਪੜੀਆ ਮਨਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਉਪਜੀ ਭਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: haath kamanddal kaaparreea man trisanaa upajee bhaaree |
lines.english: With bowl in hand, wearing his patched coat, great desires well up in his mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਰਤੁ ਨ ਮਿਟਈ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਨ ਬੂਝੈ ਪਸੂਆ ਮਾਹਿ ਸਮਾਨਾ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: kirat na mittee hukam na boojhai pasooaa maeh samaanaa |5|
lines.english: Past actions cannot be erased; without understanding the Hukam of the Lord's Command, they become beasts. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਖ ਕਰੇ ਕਰਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਨ ਚੀਨੈ ਲੰਪਟੁ ਹੈ ਬਾਜਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: sikh kare kar sabad na cheenai lanpatt hai baajaaree |
lines.english: He teaches and preaches, but does not contemplate the Shabad; he is bought and sold on the street.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸਤ੍ਰੀ ਤਜਿ ਕਰਿ ਕਾਮਿ ਵਿਆਪਿਆ ਚਿਤੁ ਲਾਇਆ ਪਰ ਨਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: eisatree taj kar kaam viaapiaa chit laaeaa par naaree |
lines.english: Abandoning his own wife, he is engrossed in sexual desire; his thoughts are on the wives of others.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਸੰਨਿਆਸੀ ਜੋ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸੇਵੈ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: so saniaasee jo satigur sevai vichahu aap gavaae |
lines.english: He alone is a Sannyaasi, who serves the True Guru, and removes his self-conceit from within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਬਿਖੁ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਨਿਭਰਾਤੀ ਤਾ ਜਮੁ ਕਰੇ ਖੁਆਰੀ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: antar bikh baahar nibharaatee taa jam kare khuaaree |6|
lines.english: With poison within, he pretends to be free of doubt; he is ruined and humiliated by the Messenger of Death. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਕੈ ਨ ਬੋਲੈ ਖਿਮਾ ਧਨੁ ਸੰਗ੍ਰਹੈ ਤਾਮਸੁ ਨਾਮਿ ਜਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: bakai na bolai khimaa dhan sangrahai taamas naam jalaae |
lines.english: He does not speak empty words; he gathers in the wealth of tolerance, and burns away his anger with the Naam.

lines.gurmukhi: ਛਾਦਨ ਭੋਜਨ ਕੀ ਆਸ ਨ ਕਰਈ ਅਚਿੰਤੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਸੋ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: chhaadan bhojan kee aas na karee achint milai so paae |
lines.english: He does not ask for clothes or food; without asking, he accepts whatever he receives.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਗਿਰਹੀ ਸੰਨਿਆਸੀ ਜੋਗੀ ਜਿ ਹਰਿ ਚਰਣੀ ਚਿਤੁ ਲਾਏ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: dhan girahee saniaasee jogee ji har charanee chit laae |7|
lines.english: Blessed is such a householder, Sannyaasi and Yogi, who focuses his consciousness on the Lord's feet. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਰਹੈ ਸੰਨਿਆਸੀ ਏਕਸੁ ਸਿਉ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: aas niraas rahai saniaasee ekas siau liv laae |
lines.english: Amidst hope, the Sannyaasi remains unmoved by hope; he remains lovingly focused on the One Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਪੀਵੈ ਤਾ ਸਾਤਿ ਆਵੈ ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਤਾੜੀ ਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: har ras peevai taa saat aavai nij ghar taarree laae |
lines.english: He drinks in the sublime essence of the Lord, and so finds peace and tranquility; in the home of his own being, he remains absorbed in the deep trance of meditation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੂਆ ਨ ਡੋਲੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੂਝੈ ਧਾਵਤੁ ਵਰਜਿ ਰਹਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: manooaa na ddolai guramukh boojhai dhaavat varaj rahaae |
lines.english: His mind does not waver; as Gurmukh, he understands. He restrains it from wandering out.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗ੍ਰਿਹੁ ਸਰੀਰੁ ਗੁਰਮਤੀ ਖੋਜੇ ਨਾਮੁ ਪਦਾਰਥੁ ਪਾਏ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: grihu sareer guramatee khoje naam padaarath paae |8|
lines.english: Following the Guru's Teachings, he searches the home of his body, and obtains the wealth of the Naam. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਿ ਸੁਖ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨਾਮ ਧੁਨਿ ਬਾਣੀ ਸਚੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਉਰ ਧਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: sabh sukh mukat naam dhun baanee sach naam ur dhaaree |
lines.english: All comforts and liberation are found in the Naam, and the vibrations of the Guru's Bani; I have enshrined the True Name within my heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਬਿਸਨੁ ਮਹੇਸੁ ਸਰੇਸਟ ਨਾਮਿ ਰਤੇ ਵੀਚਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: brahamaa bisan mahes saresatt naam rate veechaaree |
lines.english: Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva are exalted, imbued with contemplative meditation on the Naam.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਾਣੀ ਬਾਣੀ ਗਗਨ ਪਤਾਲੀ ਜੰਤਾ ਜੋਤਿ ਤੁਮਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: khaanee baanee gagan pataalee jantaa jot tumaaree |
lines.english: The sources of creation, speech, the heavens and the underworld, all beings and creatures, are infused with Your Light.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨਾ ਨਹੀ ਛੂਟਸਿ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਾਚੀ ਤਰੁ ਤੂ ਤਾਰੀ ॥੯॥੭॥
lines.translit: naam binaa nahee chhoottas naanak saachee tar too taaree |9|7|
lines.english: Without the Naam, no one is saved; O Nanak, with the Truth, cross over to the other side. ||9||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਗਰਭ ਉਰਧਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗੀ ਸੋ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਸਾਰੇ ਦਾਤਿ ਕਰੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: antar garabh uradh liv laagee so prabh saare daat kare |1|
lines.english: Upside-down within the womb, it lovingly dwells on the Lord; God provides for it, and gives it nourishment there. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Maaroo, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਤ ਪਿਤਾ ਸੰਜੋਗਿ ਉਪਾਏ ਰਕਤੁ ਬਿੰਦੁ ਮਿਲਿ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਕਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: maat pitaa sanjog upaae rakat bind mil pindd kare |
lines.english: Through the union of mother and father, the fetus is formed. The egg and sperm join together to make the body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਸਾਰੁ ਭਵਜਲੁ ਕਿਉ ਤਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: sansaar bhavajal kiau tarai |
lines.english: How can he cross over the terrifying world-ocean?

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੁ ਪਾਈਐ ਅਫਰਿਓ ਭਾਰੁ ਅਫਾਰੁ ਟਰੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh naam niranjan paaeeai afario bhaar afaar ttarai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The Gurmukh obtains the Immaculate Naam, the Name of the Lord; the unbearable load of sins is removed. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇ ਗੁਣ ਵਿਸਰਿ ਗਏ ਅਪਰਾਧੀ ਮੈ ਬਉਰਾ ਕਿਆ ਕਰਉ ਹਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: te gun visar ge aparaadhee mai bauraa kiaa krau hare |
lines.english: I have forgotten Your Virtues, Lord; I am insane - what can I do now?

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਾਰਿ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਲੈ ਜਗਿ ਜਨਮਿਆ ਸਿਵ ਸਕਤੀ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸੁ ਧਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: chaar padaarath lai jag janamiaa siv sakatee ghar vaas dhare |
lines.english: One is born to achieve the four great objectives of life. The spirit has taken up its home in the material world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਦਾਤਾ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਸਭੈ ਸਿਰਿ ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਦਾਤਿ ਸਮਾਰਿ ਕਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: too daataa deaal sabhai sir ahinis daat samaar kare |2|
lines.english: You are the Merciful Giver, above the heads of all. Day and night, You give gifts, and take care of all. ||2||
